name: German Ice Cream Torte
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces frozen red raspberries (thawed and drained)
1/2 cup whipping cream, whipped and chilled
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the rim from a 9-inch springform pan. Cut brown paper to fit the bottom.
2. Beat egg whites and cream of tartar until foamy. Slowly beat in superfine sugar, beating until stiff. Spread evenly on paper.
3. Bake for 50 minutes at 275°F (140°C). Turn off heat. Leave meringue in the oven with the door closed for 1 hour.
4. Remove paper. Return meringue to the bottom of the pan. Replace the rim.
5. Mix gelatin with cold water in the top of a double boiler. Let stand for 5 minutes.
6. Add egg yolks, sugar, and sherry. Beat over simmering water until thickened.
7. Cool. Fold the egg mixture into the whipped cream.
8. Divide into 3 equal parts. Add cocoa to one portion, raspberries to another, and leave the third plain.
9. Spread the cocoa mixture evenly over the meringue. Freeze for 10 minutes.
10. Top with an even layer of the raspberry mixture. Freeze for 10 more minutes.
11. Cover with the plain mixture and freeze for 8 hours.
12. To serve, remove the rim and transfer the tart to a serving plate. Shave unsweetened chocolate over the top and sides of the tart.
13. Ring the top of the tart with 12 mounds or rosettes of whipped cream. Top each with a candy kiss.
14. Return the wrapped tart to the freezer until 10 minutes before serving. Cut into wedges.
